title: prunus × lannesiana f. juzukakezakura
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. juzukakezakura, also known as the Juzuka cherry, is a cultivar of cherry blossom tree that is native to Japan. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Juzuka cherry has been used to treat various health issues, including hypertension, hyperglycemia, and cardiovascular diseases, due to its ability to improve blood circulation and lower cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Prunus × lannesiana f. juzukakezakura typically involve steeping the flowers or bark in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
